pending.body: Restrict `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` so it only replays final-review aggregates that are actually committed on the current run branch; the current scan in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:865-963` accepts any `final_review` stage aggregate whose `payload_id` starts with the `run_id`, but final-review aggregate files are written before the journal commit (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2632-2669`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7499-7535`) and are also branch-scoped by `rollback_count` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7377-7390`). After a rollback, or after a crash between aggregate persistence and `stage_completed`/`completion_round_advanced`, this code will reconstruct and persist planned-elsewhere mappings from abandoned or never-committed review attempts.

Do not treat a closed mapped-to bead as a stale reference in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/planned_elsewhere.rs:60-80`. The new `verify_bead_exists` helper currently marks `BeadStatus::Closed` as `Stale`, which means a legitimate planned-elsewhere classification pointing at already-completed work is downgraded to a warning and will never be marked verified.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 12 -> Plan and Implement
